topic: Comparing histograms and analyzing distributions for two data sets
latex:
The frequency table shows the ages of the members of two local fitness clubs.

\begin{center}
\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
\textbf{Ages of Members} & \textbf{18--25} & \textbf{26--33} & \textbf{34--41} & \textbf{42--49} & \textbf{50--57} & \textbf{58--65} \\
\hline
Frequency for Fitness Club A & 14 & 19 & 13 & 8 & 6 & 3 \\
\hline
Frequency for Fitness Club B & 4 & 9 & 12 & 13 & 8 & 5 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{center}

\textbf{a.} Display the data for each fitness club in a histogram. Describe the shape of each distribution.

\textbf{b.} Which fitness club would appeal more to a young adult? Explain.
